brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Good. Now the one that matters. Same join — filter to chrono-portal-mirror.

expected.sql: SELECT timestamp, message FROM logs JOIN services ON logs.tags['service'] = service_name WHERE logs.tags['service'] = 'chrono-portal-mirror' LIMIT 10

hints.text: Carol: 'That service is registered. We're looking for the one that isn't.'
Carol: 'Query error. Filter on the service tag — tags[\'service\'] = \'chrono-portal-mirror\'.'
Carol: 'tags[\'service\'] = \'chrono-portal-mirror\' — map notation for the service tag.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text: Zero rows. An inner join only returns rows that match on both sides. No match means no rows. The service exists in logs. It just doesn't exist in the catalog.
